---
title: "Geometric Objects"
format:
  html:
    code-tools: true
jupyter: plotnine-fluid
---

This notebook adapts the plotnine guide on geometric objects to the fluent API
style. Where the original page uses pandas-based wrangling, this version uses
Polars.

```{python}
import polars as pl
import plotnine as p9
from plotnine import ggplot, aes
from plotnine.data import diamonds, huron, mpg
```

## Setup

```{python}
mpg = pl.from_pandas(mpg)
huron = pl.from_pandas(huron)
diamonds = pl.from_pandas(diamonds)

mpg.head()
```

## Basic Use

Geom functions determine how mapped data becomes visible marks. Layers are drawn
in the order they are added.

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("displ", "hwy")
    .geom_point(aes(fill="class"), size=5)
    .geom_point(aes(shape="class"))
)
```

## Individual Geoms

### Scatterplot With Text

```{python}
highest_mpg = (
    mpg
    .filter(
        pl.col("hwy") == pl.col("hwy").max(), 
        pl.col("cty") == pl.col("cty").max()
    )
)

(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("cty", "hwy")
    .geom_point()
    .geom_text(
        aes(label="model"),
        nudge_y=-2,
        nudge_x=-1,
        data=highest_mpg,
    )
)
```

### Barchart on Counts

```{python}
(
    mpg
    .group_by("class", "drv").len()
    .rename({"len": "count"})
    >>
    ggplot()
    .aes("class", "count", fill="drv")
    .geom_col()
)
```

### Rectangles

```{python}
(
    pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "xmin": [1, 2, 3],
            "ymin": [1, 2, 3],
            "xmax": [2, 3, 4],
            "ymax": [2, 3, 4],
        }
    )
    >>
    ggplot()
    .aes(xmin="xmin", ymin="ymin", xmax="xmax", ymax="ymax")
    .geom_rect(fill="orange")
)
```

## Collective Geoms for Distributions

### Boxplots and Violins

```{python}
selected_classes = ["2seater", "compact", "midsize"]
mpg_box = mpg.filter(pl.col("class").is_in(selected_classes))
mpg_violin = mpg.filter(~pl.col("class").is_in(selected_classes))

(
    ggplot()
    .aes("class", "cty")
    .geom_boxplot(data=mpg_box, fill="orange")
    .geom_violin(data=mpg_violin, fill="lightblue")
)
```

### Histograms and Densities

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("cty")
    .geom_histogram()
)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("cty")
    .geom_density(fill="lightblue")
)
```

### Smoothing

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("displ", "hwy")
    .geom_point()
    .geom_smooth(method="lm", color="blue", fill="orange")
)
```

## Collective Geoms for Lines and Fills

```{python}
(
    ggplot(huron)
    .aes("year", "level")
    .geom_line()
    .geom_point()
)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(huron)
    .aes("year", "level")
    .geom_ribbon(aes(ymax="level"), ymin=0, fill="lightblue")
    .geom_point()
)
```

## Position Adjustments

### Jitter With Random Noise

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("cty", "hwy")
    .geom_point(alpha=0.2)
)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("cty", "hwy")
    .geom_point(position=p9.position_jitter())
)
```

### Dodge and Fill for Bars

```{python}
(
    ggplot(diamonds)
    .aes("color", fill="cut")
    .add_theme(legend_position="none")
    .geom_bar()
)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(diamonds)
    .aes("color", fill="cut")
    .add_theme(legend_position="none")
    .geom_bar(position=p9.position_fill())
)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(diamonds)
    .aes("color", fill="cut")
    .add_theme(legend_position="none")
    .geom_bar(position=p9.position_dodge())
)
```

## Statistical Transformations

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("trans", "cty")
    .geom_point()
    .geom_point(
        aes(group="trans"),
        color="red",
        size=3,
        stat="summary",
        fun_y=lambda x: x.mean(),
    )
)
```

```{python}
mean_mpg = mpg.group_by("trans").agg(pl.col("cty").mean())
mean_mpg
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(mpg)
    .aes("trans", "cty")
    .geom_point()
    .geom_point(data=mean_mpg, color="red", size=3)
)
```
